annotations.label: Inner product space

anchor.snippet: an inner product space

annotations.note: The class `InnerProductSpace 𝕜 E` (with `[RCLike 𝕜]`) is exactly Mathlib's notion of inner product space.

annotations.label: Hilbert space (as complete inner product space)

anchor.snippet: If this normed space is also complete

annotations.note: The `variable_alias` structure `HilbertSpace 𝕜 E` bundles `InnerProductSpace` with `CompleteSpace`.

annotations.label: Inner product (formal definition)

anchor.snippet: An inner product space is a vector space

annotations.note: The `Inner 𝕜 E` class provides the inner product operation used by `InnerProductSpace`.

anchors.snippet: An inner product space is a vector space

annotations.label: Conjugate symmetry

anchor.snippet: Conjugate symmetry

annotations.note: The axiom `conj_inner_symm` is a field of `InnerProductSpace` and is exposed as the theorem `inner_conj_symm`.

annotations.label: Linearity in the first argument

anchor.snippet: Linearity in the first argument

annotations.note: Mathlib's `add_left` + `smul_left` fields encode sesquilinearity (conjugate-linear in first argument), the same content modulo convention.

anchors.snippet: Linearity in the first argument

annotations.label: Positive-definiteness

anchor.snippet: Positive-definiteness

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.Core` requires `re_inner_nonneg` and `definite`, yielding positive-definiteness.

annotations.label: Positive semi-definite Hermitian form is an inner product iff

anchor.snippet: A positive semi-definite Hermitian form

annotations.note: Mathlib distinguishes `PreInnerProductSpace.Core` (semi-definite) from `InnerProductSpace.Core` (adds `definite`), but does not state the explicit iff characterization.

annotations.label: Inner product is sesquilinear (conjugate-linearity in 2nd argument)

anchor.snippet: that is conjugate-linearity

annotations.note: `inner_add_right` and `inner_smul_right` (with `inner_conj_symm`) give conjugate-linearity in the second slot under Mathlib's convention.

annotations.label: Real inner product is positive-definite symmetric bilinear form

anchor.snippet: Hence an inner product on a real vector space is a positive-definite symmetric bilinear form

annotations.note: Over ℝ, `real_inner_comm` plus `inner_add_left/inner_smul_left` plus `real_inner_self_eq_norm_mul_norm` yield a symmetric positive-definite bilinear form.

anchors.snippet: Hence an inner product on a real vector space is a positive-definite symmetric bilinear form

annotations.label: Real numbers as inner product space

anchor.snippet: The real numbers

annotations.note: The instance `RCLike.innerProductSpace` makes ℝ an inner product space over itself.

anchors.snippet: The real numbers

annotations.label: Complex numbers as inner product space

anchor.snippet: The complex numbers

annotations.note: Same `RCLike.innerProductSpace` instance covers ℂ (since `RCLike ℂ`).

anchors.snippet: The complex numbers

annotations.label: Euclidean vector space with dot product

anchor.snippet: with the dot product is an inner product space

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace 𝕜 ι` is the standard L²/dot-product inner product space on `ι → 𝕜`.

anchors.snippet: with the dot product is an inner product space

annotations.label: Characterization of inner products on R^n via symmetric positive-definite matrices

anchor.snippet: is an inner product on

annotations.note: `Matrix.toInnerProductSpace` constructs the inner product on `n → 𝕜` induced by a positive-(semi)definite matrix.

anchors.snippet: is an inner product on

annotations.label: Hermitian form on complex coordinate space

anchor.snippet: The general form of an inner product

annotations.note: `Matrix.toInnerProductSpace` (over `RCLike 𝕜 = ℂ`) gives the general `⟪x, y⟫ = xᴴ M y` form on `n → ℂ`.

anchors.snippet: The general form of an inner product

annotations.label: Space of continuous functions with L^2 inner product (incomplete)

anchor.snippet: An example of an inner product space which induces an incomplete metric

annotations.note: Mathlib formalizes the (complete) `Lp E 2 μ` inner product space, but does not single out the incomplete subspace of continuous functions.

anchors.snippet: An example of an inner product space which induces an incomplete metric

annotations.label: Expectation as inner product on random variables

anchor.snippet: the expected value of their product

annotations.note: On `Lp E 2 μ` the inner product is `∫ ⟪f, g⟫ dμ`, which specializes to `E[XY]`; no dedicated random-variable inner product instance exists.

anchors.snippet: the expected value of their product

annotations.label: Frobenius inner product on complex matrices

anchor.snippet: The inner product for complex square matrices

annotations.note: Mathlib has matrix-induced inner products and Matrix.trace, and EuclideanSpace gives a Frobenius-like form, but there is no dedicated `Matrix.frobeniusInner` decl.

anchors.snippet: The inner product for complex square matrices

annotations.label: Canonical norm induced by inner product

anchor.snippet: induces a norm , called its canonical norm

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.Core.toNormedAddCommGroup` defines `‖x‖ = √(re ⟪x,x⟫)`; `norm_sq_eq_re_inner` enforces this on `InnerProductSpace`.

annotations.label: Cauchy–Schwarz inequality

anchor.snippet: with equality if and only if

annotations.note: `norm_inner_le_norm` proves `‖⟪x,y⟫‖ ≤ ‖x‖ * ‖y‖`; equality case is not stated as a single iff.

anchors.snippet: with equality if and only if

annotations.label: Parallelogram law characterizes inner-product norms

anchor.snippet: The parallelogram law is a necessary and sufficient condition

annotations.note: `InnerProductSpace.ofNorm` constructs an inner product from a norm satisfying the parallelogram identity (`InnerProductSpaceable`), the necessary-and-sufficient direction.

anchors.snippet: The parallelogram law is a necessary and sufficient condition

annotations.label: Polarization identity

anchor.snippet: The inner product can be retrieved from the norm by the polarization identity

annotations.note: Mathlib has a family of polarization identities (`re_inner_eq_norm_add_mul_self_sub_norm_sub_mul_self_div_four`, `inner_eq_sum_norm_sq_div_four`).

anchors.snippet: The inner product can be retrieved from the norm by the polarization identity

annotations.label: Ptolemy's inequality characterizes inner-product norms

anchor.snippet: Ptolemy's inequality is a necessary and sufficient condition

annotations.note: Mathlib proves Ptolemy's inequality in Euclidean geometry but not the characterization that Ptolemy's inequality detects inner-product norms among general normed spaces.

anchors.snippet: Ptolemy's inequality is a necessary and sufficient condition

annotations.label: Orthogonal vectors

anchor.snippet: are said to be orthogonal

annotations.note: Orthogonality of two vectors is `⟪x, y⟫ = 0`; Mathlib has lemmas like `inner_eq_zero_symm` and `Submodule.isOrtho` for sets.

anchors.snippet: are said to be orthogonal

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

annotations.label: Operator vanishing characterization via inner product (complex case)

anchor.snippet: a linear operator

annotations.note: `inner_map_polarization` plus the fact that `T = 0 ↔ ∀ x, ⟪T x, x⟫ = 0` over ℂ is established (cf. `Symmetric.lean` and `LinearMap.lean`).

anchors.snippet: a linear operator

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

annotations.label: Orthogonal complement

anchor.snippet: The orthogonal complement of a subset

annotations.note: `Submodule.orthogonal K` (notated `Kᗮ`) is the orthogonal complement of `K`.

anchors.snippet: The orthogonal complement of a subset

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem

anchor.snippet: The name Pythagorean theorem arises

annotations.note: Pythagorean theorem `‖x+y‖² = ‖x‖² + ‖y‖²` from `⟪x,y⟫ = 0` is directly available.

anchors.snippet: The name Pythagorean theorem arises

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

annotations.label: Pythagorean theorem for pairwise orthogonal vectors

anchor.snippet: An induction on the Pythagorean theorem yields

annotations.note: Mathlib's `OrthogonalFamily` / `Orthonormal.norm_sum_sq`-style lemmas extend Pythagoras to finite orthogonal sums.

anchors.snippet: An induction on the Pythagorean theorem yields

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

annotations.label: Angle between vectors via Cauchy–Schwarz

anchor.snippet: the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality implies

annotations.note: `InnerProductGeometry.angle` defines the undirected angle via `arccos (⟪x,y⟫ / (‖x‖‖y‖))`, well-defined thanks to Cauchy–Schwarz.

anchors.snippet: the Cauchy–Schwarz inequality implies

anchors.value: {\displaystyle C^{\bot }:=\{\,y\in V:\langle y,c\rangle =0{\text{ for all }}c\in C\,\}.}

anchor.snippet: The polarization identity shows that the real part

annotations.label: Polarization identity for complex inner products

anchor.section: Real and complex parts of inner products
anchor.snippet: The polarization identity for complex vector spaces

annotations.note: `inner_eq_sum_norm_sq_div_four` expresses the full complex inner product as a sum of four norms over 𝕜.

anchors.section: Real and complex parts of inner products
anchors.snippet: The polarization identity for complex vector spaces

annotations.label: Complex inner product determined by its real part

anchor.section: Real and complex parts of inner products
anchor.snippet: every complex inner product is completely determined by its real part

annotations.note: Mathlib defines `Inner.rclikeToReal` taking `re ⟪·,·⟫` and recovers `⟪x,y⟫` via polarization, but no single decl states the uniqueness theorem.

annotations.label: Complex inner product induced by dot product on C^n

anchor.section: Real and complex parts of inner products
anchor.snippet: The unique complex inner product

annotations.note: `EuclideanSpace ℂ (Fin n)` is exactly the standard inner product on `ℂⁿ`.

annotations.label: Real part of complex inner product is real inner product

anchor.snippet: which necessarily forms a real inner product

annotations.note: The definition `Inner.rclikeToReal` and the resulting `InnerProductSpace ℝ E` instance turn a complex IP space into a real one via the real part.

annotations.label: 90° rotation counterexample on real inner product space

anchor.snippet: this rotation map

annotations.note: No dedicated decl gives this specific counterexample; the related `Orientation.rightAngleRotation` exists but is not framed as a counterexample to the real-vs-complex inner product distinction.

annotations.label: Orthonormal basis (finite-dimensional)

anchor.snippet: a basis

annotations.note: `OrthonormalBasis ι 𝕜 E` is Mathlib's finite-index orthonormal basis.

annotations.label: Orthonormal basis (infinite-dimensional)

anchor.snippet: This definition of orthonormal basis generalizes

annotations.note: `HilbertBasis ι 𝕜 E` is an isometric isomorphism `E ≃ₗᵢ ℓ²(ι, 𝕜)`, the infinite-dimensional orthonormal basis.

anchors.snippet: This definition of orthonormal basis generalizes

annotations.label: Separable inner product space has orthonormal basis

anchor.snippet: Any separable inner product space has an orthonormal basis

annotations.note: Mathlib has `exists_orthonormalBasis` only in finite dimensions; no general existence theorem for separable Hilbert spaces is stated.

annotations.label: Complete inner product space has orthonormal basis

anchor.snippet: Any complete inner product space has an orthonormal basis

annotations.note: `exists_orthonormalBasis` covers the finite-dimensional case; the general Zorn-based existence of a `HilbertBasis` in any Hilbert space is not formalized.

annotations.label: Parseval isometry for separable inner product spaces

anchor.snippet: is an isometric linear map

annotations.note: `HilbertBasis.repr` is by definition a `LinearIsometryEquiv` between `E` and `ℓ²(ι, 𝕜)`, giving Parseval's identity.

anchors.snippet: is an isometric linear map

annotations.label: Fourier series orthonormal basis on L^2

anchor.snippet: is an orthonormal basis of the space

annotations.note: `fourierBasis : HilbertBasis ℤ ℂ (Lp ℂ 2 haarAddCircle)` is exactly the orthonormal Fourier basis.

anchors.snippet: is an orthonormal basis of the space

annotations.label: Continuous linear maps between inner product spaces

anchor.snippet: Continuous linear maps

annotations.note: `E →L[𝕜] F` is the generic continuous linear map class; specialized usage on inner product spaces is throughout `Mathlib.Analysis.InnerProductSpace`.

annotations.label: Symmetric linear operator

anchor.snippet: Symmetric linear operators

annotations.note: `LinearMap.IsSymmetric T` says `∀ x y, ⟪T x, y⟫ = ⟪x, T y⟫`, matching the Wikipedia definition.

annotations.label: Isometry between inner product spaces

anchor.snippet: Isometries

annotations.note: `LinearIsometry 𝕜 E F` (notation `E →ₗᵢ[𝕜] F`) is the norm-preserving linear map; `LinearIsometry.inner_map_map` gives inner-product preservation.

annotations.label: Mazur–Ulam theorem for isometries

anchor.snippet: The Mazur–Ulam theorem establishes

annotations.note: The Mazur–Ulam theorem is in `Mathlib.Analysis.Normed.Affine.MazurUlam`, asserting surjective isometries between normed real spaces are affine.

annotations.label: Isometrical isomorphism / unitary operator

anchor.snippet: Isometrical isomorphisms

annotations.note: `LinearIsometryEquiv` (notation `E ≃ₗᵢ[𝕜] F`) is the surjective linear isometry, i.e. the unitary in the inner-product setting.

annotations.label: Spectral theorem

anchor.snippet: The spectral theorem provides a canonical form

annotations.note: `LinearMap.IsSymmetric.diagonalization` gives the spectral / diagonalization theorem for self-adjoint operators on finite-dimensional inner product spaces.

annotations.label: Degenerate inner product / semi-norm quotient construction

anchor.snippet: a semi-definite sesquilinear form

annotations.note: `PreInnerProductSpace.Core` formalizes the semi-definite Hermitian form yielding a seminorm, but the explicit quotient-by-radical construction is not packaged.

anchors.snippet: a semi-definite sesquilinear form

annotations.label: Nondegenerate conjugate symmetric form

anchor.snippet: one may require that the pairing be a nondegenerate form

annotations.note: Nondegeneracy is formalized at the level of bilinear/sesquilinear forms (`BilinForm.Nondegenerate`), but not as a dedicated 'nondegenerate Hermitian form' structure.

annotations.label: Sylvester's law of inertia

anchor.snippet: By Sylvester's law of inertia

annotations.note: Sylvester's law of inertia (uniqueness of the signature) is proved in `Mathlib.LinearAlgebra.QuadraticForm.Signature`.

annotations.label: Minkowski space as indefinite inner product

anchor.snippet: Product of vectors in Minkowski space

annotations.note: Mathlib does not define Minkowski space or its indefinite inner product form.
